message:
chore(diagnostic, outcome): align test fixtures with struct field + Option<String> API evolution

Two small test-side updates tracking API changes that landed in
non-test code elsewhere in the workspace.

## diagnostic.rs: DiagnosticOpts now carries include_full_cwd

Two test-constructor sites for `DiagnosticOpts` were missing the
newly-added `include_full_cwd: bool` field. The field got added in
non-test code (diagnostic options plumbing — tracked separately) and
the struct literal construction in tests fails to compile without
it. Set the field to `false` in both test sites, matching the
conservative default of "don't include full cwd unless explicitly
opted in":

    DiagnosticOpts {
        event_limit: 10,
        ...
        output: Some(PathBuf::from("/tmp/custom")),
        include_full_cwd: false,    // <- added
    }

Second site is in the clone/assertion test that builds a DiagnosticOpts,
clones it, modifies the clone, and asserts the original is unchanged —
same field addition, same default.

## outcome.rs: cancel_user message is now Option<String>

cancel_user() used to return a struct whose `message` field was
Option<&'static str>. It's been widened to Option<String> (likely
to support dynamic reason strings at the call site). The one test
site that asserts against the literal still compares against
`Some("user stopped")`:

    - assert_eq!(r.message, Some("user stopped"));
    + assert_eq!(r.message.as_deref(), Some("user stopped"));

Using `.as_deref()` converts Option<String> to Option<&str> for the
comparison against the &'static str literal, so the assertion
succeeds without an unnecessary String allocation on the test side.

No production code changed in this commit — just two struct-literal
completions and one comparison-ergonomics adjustment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
